prompt: {P1} is interviewing {P2} for a job. {P1} secretly does not want to hire anyone.

{P1} is a doctor and {P2} is the patient. {P2} is convinced nothing is wrong with them.
{P1} is a real estate agent desperately trying to sell {P2} a terrible house.
{P1} and {P2} have both been assigned the same airplane seat. The flight leaves in 2 minutes.
{P1} is a cooking show host and {P2} is their guest who has secretly never cooked anything before.
{P1} and {P2} have been lost in IKEA for 3 hours and are starting to lose their minds.
{P1} is a superhero. {P2} is their sidekick who is much better at the job but too polite to say so.
{P1} is a museum tour guide who knows nothing about art. {P2} is a very suspicious visitor.
{P1} gives a passionate motivational speech to the audience about why socks are the most important thing in the world.
{P1} stars in a live infomercial selling an ordinary household item as if it is revolutionary.
{P1} is a news reporter live on the scene of a very minor and boring local event.
{P1} delivers an Oscar acceptance speech for a film they made entirely by accident.
{P1} films a tutorial video explaining how to do something they have clearly never done before.
{P1} calls in to a radio show to defend their unusual life choices in front of a live audience.
{P1} and {P2} are neighbors having an argument about something increasingly petty.
{P1} is recording a time capsule video for people 100 years in the future. {P2} keeps interrupting.
{P1} and {P2} are trapped in an escape room. They have found the solution but disagree on how to use it.
{P1} and {P2} are stuck in an elevator together. They have never met. They will be here for a while.
{P1} is an experienced wilderness survival expert. {P2} booked what they thought was a luxury spa weekend.
